content: Personally I am concerned that four Reviewed-bys isn't enough for adding two backticks, will there be any more? :smile: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/27211D21-4A09-48B0-B51F-1C353BEDC784@kloenk.dev/T/#t
If you add your Reviewed-by, then we'll have five.

Trevor Gross said:

Personally I am concerned that four Reviewed-bys isn't enough for adding two backticks, will there be any more? :smile: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/27211D21-4A09-48B0-B51F-1C353BEDC784@kloenk.dev/T/#t

If I were to say I think this PR is kinda pointless if we consider that SAFETY comments don't get rendered for any doc and it's also a plain comment.
As a kernel newbie, does i have the right to send the message mail containing Reviewed-by? just send it for the above patch.

Martin Rodriguez Reboredo said:

Trevor Gross said:

Personally I am concerned that four Reviewed-bys isn't enough for adding two backticks, will there be any more? :smile: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/27211D21-4A09-48B0-B51F-1C353BEDC784@kloenk.dev/T/#t

If I were to say I think this PR is kinda pointless if we consider that SAFETY comments don't get rendered for any doc and it's also a plain comment.

technically I think the style guide says that comments should be written in MD style for consistency, but yeah it's not really needed
I wonder what the most reviewed commit is in the kernel

baul said:

As a kernel newbie, does i have the right to send the message mail containing Reviewed-by? just send it for the above patch.

You just need the message before the --- or even just the existing SoB/Rb/CaBs in the commit message I think. Of course this one line patch doesn't matter in the slightest
But from the submitter viewpoint, it is import step to enter into the kernel community. It is an encouragent. but maybe kinda pointless from other . So need a tradeoff?

Trevor Gross said:

Martin Rodriguez Reboredo said:

Trevor Gross said:

Personally I am concerned that four Reviewed-bys isn't enough for adding two backticks, will there be any more? :smile: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/27211D21-4A09-48B0-B51F-1C353BEDC784@kloenk.dev/T/#t

If I were to say I think this PR is kinda pointless if we consider that SAFETY comments don't get rendered for any doc and it's also a plain comment.

technically I think the style guide says that comments should be written in MD style for consistency, but yeah it's not really needed

baul said:

But from the submitter viewpoint, it is import step to enter into the kernel community. It is an encouragent. but maybe kinda pointless from other . So need a tradeoff?

If something meaningful was intended then I'd rather say that this MarkDown SAFETY styling should be applied to more files too. A oneliner patch for a typo that it's outside of documentation is not that much IMO.

In my humble opinion, there is no such thing as a pointless patch (like this conversation is); everyone has to start somewhere. As is often the case in all projects, simple patches tend to receive more reviews. Perhaps reviews should skip over the simple patches if they have already been reviewed? because at this point the review is pointless not the patch. If you are fixing this code in a patch series it should be in its own commit so the trivial patch is always present.

The 'Review-by' tag also serves to inform kernel developers. It signals that I am familiar with the changes, so if something goes wrong and breaks things, I know how to fix it. In this case, having more than one 'Review-by' might seem redundant from a review perspective.

However, it's not redundant from the author's perspective because this process teaches them how to contribute to the Rust subsystem. I am aware that the USB subsystem has different rules regarding patch title formatting and other aspects and also a simple patch that fix a typo can teach something to someone (e.g: if you are not familiar with English, this process teaches you in what form write your commit).
